repo.name: fossasia/visdom
repo.sha_provenance: default-branch (dev) HEAD at or before the 2026-06-10 validation date, resolved via GitHub API 2026-06-11 (the validation clone was not retained; the file:line citations below are the durable anchors and are SHA-robust because they describe structural conformance to a slow-changing documented rule).

documented_rule: "All handlers must use `@check_auth` decorator" — .agents/skills/websocket-flow/SKILL.md:44 (under the '## Guardrails' bullet list); reinforced at .agents/skills/websocket-flow/SKILL.md:31 ('Implement post() with @check_auth decorator' in the 'Adding a New API Endpoint' how-to).
rule_location_kind: other (skill-guardrail how-to doc, not a formal ADR)

census.class_selector: HTTP request-handler method entry points (def get|post on route-registered handler classes in py/visdom/server/handlers/web_handlers.py); excludes the abstract BaseHandler/BaseWebSocketHandler and WebSocket on_message/open handlers (which auth via a different mechanism). No test/generated/vendored files in scope.
census.conformance_check: the @check_auth decorator is present immediately above the get/post method (structural fact: decorator token on the line(s) preceding the def).
deviants_sample: py/visdom/server/handlers/web_handlers.py:652 (IndexHandler.post — the LOGIN endpoint; cannot require auth to authenticate)
py/visdom/server/handlers/web_handlers.py:628 (IndexHandler.get — serves login.html / index, does its own login_enabled/current_user check inline)
py/visdom/server/handlers/web_handlers.py:693 (HealthHandler.get — health probe; registered in app.py:123 WITHOUT an app dict, intentionally unauthenticated)
census.signal_reason: Rule is HARD ('must'). k=13 >= ceil(2N/3)=ceil(36/3)=12 AND k=13 > N-k=5, so the math yields CORROBORATE (strong majority conforms; deviants stand out structurally). The signal is arithmetically correct as a rule-health/outlier flag. CRITICAL CAVEAT for downstream judgment: although the 5 deviants are real structural non-conformers, every one is a LEGITIMATELY-unauthenticated endpoint (login form+handler, static CSS, error page, health probe), not a defect — so CORROBORATE correctly identifies outliers but those outliers must NOT be auto-judged as violations. This is exactly why CORROBORATE is an overlay signal, not an auto-finding.

naive_vs_guarded.genuine_deviation_found: none — clean repo. All 5 structural deviants are by-design unauthenticated endpoints, not violations: IndexHandler.post (web_handlers.py:652) is the login endpoint itself (auth-to-login is impossible); IndexHandler.get (:628) gates on login_enabled/current_user inline and renders login.html; HealthHandler.get (:693) is a health probe registered without an app dict (app.py:123); UserSettingsHandler.get (:670) serves static style.css; ErrorHandler.get (:678) renders the error page. There is no hard-rule, majority-followed convention with a lone defect-outlier here.

honest_assessment: The witness IS a hard, class-ranging deontic ('All handlers MUST use @check_auth'), but it lives in a how-to skill guardrail (.agents/skills/websocket-flow/SKILL.md), not a formal ADR — and it carries IMPLICIT inherent exceptions the prose doesn't enumerate. Census math is sound: N=18 method entry points, k=13 with @check_auth, 5 deviants -> CORROBORATE (13 >= 12 and 13 > 5). At the class-level framing it is N=15 classes / k=11 / 4 deviant classes -> still CORROBORATE. Either way the signal is a correct OUTLIER/rule-health flag, but it is NOT a defect list: a NAIVE detector that emitted 'missing @check_auth = violation' would fire 5 FALSE POSITIVES, all of them endpoints that MUST be unauthenticated (you cannot require auth to reach the login endpoint; a health probe and static CSS are intentionally open). The guarded IF-4 correctly stays silent: the rule has documented/inherent exceptions and the deviants diverge by-design rather than contradict, so precision-first suppression is right. Net: this validates the scope-honesty claim — a hard class-ranging deontic exists, but its 'deviants' are exceptions, not bugs; the value here is the rule-health overlay (CORROBORATE that the convention is real + near-universal), not an auto-finding. Recall: zero genuine defects to surface — the repo is clean against this rule.
verdict: CORROBORATE — clean repo; 5 naive false-positives avoided; 0 false defects emitted; census signal correct.
